Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: a continuous dose of pazopanib (800 mg once daily)
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: "We randomly assigned 1110 patients with clear-cell, metastatic renal-cell carcinoma, in a 1:1 ratio, to receive a continuous dose of pazopanib (800 mg once daily; 557 patients) or sunitinib in 6-week cycles (50 mg once daily for 4 weeks, followed by 2 weeks without treatment; 553 patients)." The first mentioned treatment arm is pazopanib with the specified regimen.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'We randomly assigned 1110 patients with clear-cell, metastatic renal-cell carcinoma, in a 1:1 ratio, to receive a continuous dose of pazopanib (800 mg once daily; 557 patients) or sunitinib in 6-week cycles (50 mg once daily for 4 weeks, followed by 2 weeks without treatment; 553 patients).'

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states: "We randomly assigned 1110 patients... to receive a continuous dose of pazopanib (800 mg once daily; 557 patients) or sunitinib in 6-week cycles (50 mg once daily for 4 weeks, followed by 2 weeks without treatment; 553 patients)." This indicates two treatment arms: pazopanib and sunitinib.

Treatment Arm(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'We randomly assigned 1110 patients... to receive a continuous dose of pazopanib (800 mg once daily; 557 patients) or sunitinib in 6-week cycles (50 mg once daily for 4 weeks, followed by 2 weeks without treatment; 553 patients).' This clearly identifies the two treatment arms as Pazopanib and Sunitinib.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'We randomly assigned 1110 patients with clear-cell, metastatic renal-cell carcinoma, in a 1:1 ratio, to receive a continuous dose of pazopanib (800 mg once daily; 557 patients) or sunitinib in 6-week cycles (50 mg once daily for 4 weeks, followed by 2 weeks without treatment; 553 patients).' This indicates a total of 1110 participants were randomized to either the treatment or control arm.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The title of the article is "Pazopanib versus Sunitinib in Metastatic Renal-Cell Carcinoma". The 'BACKGROUND' section states, "This phase 3, randomized trial compared the efficacy and safety of pazopanib and sunitinib as first-line therapy." The 'METHODS' section further clarifies, "We randomly assigned 1110 patients... to receive a continuous dose of pazopanib (800 mg once daily; 557 patients) or sunitinib...". On page 2, under 'METHODS', it states, "Among the tyrosine kinase inhibitors, pazopanib and sunitinib are first-line treatment options." Pazopanib is consistently mentioned first, indicating it as the agent in Treatment Arm 1. Therefore, the class of agent is a tyrosine kinase inhibitor.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The document reports 'median progression-free survival' (8.4 months for pazopanib, 9.5 months for sunitinib) and 'median duration of treatment' (8.0 months for pazopanib, 7.6 months for sunitinib), but not the overall 'median follow-up duration' for the entire study population in months.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: On page 5, under the 'SAFETY' section, the text states: 'The median duration of treatment was similar in the two groups: 8.0 months (range, 0 to 40) in the pazopanib group'.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: On page 5, under the 'SAFETY' section, the text states: 'The median duration of treatment was similar in the two groups: ... and 7.6 months (range, 0 to 38) in the sunitinib group'.

Control Arm.evidence: The study is a randomized, open-label, phase 3 trial comparing pazopanib versus sunitinib (page 2, 'STUDY DESIGN'). The primary objective was to show noninferiority of pazopanib versus sunitinib (page 2, 'METHODS'), indicating Sunitinib serves as the comparison group against which pazopanib's noninferiority is being assessed.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The 'METHODS' section on page 1 states, 'Secondary end points included overall survival, safety, and quality of life.' The 'RESULTS' section on page 1 also reports, 'The mean change from baseline in 11 of 14 health-related quality-of-life domains... during the first 6 months of treatment favored pazopanib (P<0.05 for all 11 comparisons).' Furthermore, a dedicated section 'HEALTH-RELATED QUALITY OF LIFE' on page 7 and 'Table 2. Change in Health-Related Quality of Life during the First 6 Months for 927 Patients Treated in the Study.' on page 8 provide detailed results on quality of life.

Control Arm - N.evidence: In the 'METHODS' section on page 1, it is stated that 'We randomly assigned 1110 patients... to receive... pazopanib (800 mg once daily; 557 patients) or sunitinib... 553 patients)'. Sunitinib is the comparator arm in this head-to-head study, with 553 participants.

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly stated in the 'CONCLUSIONS' section on page 1: "(Funded by GlaxoSmithKline Pharmaceuticals; COMPARZ ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT00720941.)"

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: overall survival, safety, and quality of life
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, the text states: 'Secondary end points included overall survival, safety, and quality of life.'

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'METHODS' section, it states: 'The primary end point was progression-free survival as assessed by independent review, and the study was powered to show the noninferiority of pazopanib versus sunitinib.' On page 2, under 'METHODS', it further clarifies: 'The primary objective was to show noninferiority of pazopanib versus sunitinib with respect to progression-free survival.' On page 3, under 'END POINTS AND ASSESSMENTS', it reiterates: 'The primary end point was progression-free survival, defined as the period between the date of randomization and the date of the first documentation of disease progression or death from any cause.'

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a report of a specific randomized controlled trial comparing pazopanib and sunitinib. It details the efficacy and safety outcomes of this particular trial but does not provide a general 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE) in randomized controlled trials (RCT)' as a broader concept or a meta-analysis.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The study is a head-to-head comparison of pazopanib versus sunitinib as first-line therapy for metastatic renal-cell carcinoma. There is no mention of any additional therapy or intervention given in combination with either of these primary treatments. The 'BACKGROUND' section states: 'This phase 3, randomized trial compared the efficacy and safety of pazopanib and sunitinib as first-line therapy.' The 'METHODS' section describes patients being assigned to receive either pazopanib or sunitinib, with no add-on treatment reported.

Treatment Class.evidence: On page 2, under the 'PAZOPANIB VS. SUNITINIB IN RENAL-CELL CARCINOMA' section, it states: 'Among the tyrosine kinase inhibitors, pazopanib and sunitinib are first-line treatment options.' This categorizes the treatments.

Type of Therapy.value: Pazopanib (800 mg once daily, continuous dosing) or Sunitinib (50 mg once daily for 4 weeks, followed by 2 weeks without treatment, in 6-week cycles). Both are first-line therapies for metastatic renal-cell carcinoma.
Type of Therapy.evidence: The 'BACKGROUND' section on page 1 states: 'This phase 3, randomized trial compared the efficacy and safety of pazopanib and sunitinib as first-line therapy.' The 'METHODS' section on page 1 further details: 'We randomly assigned 1110 patients with clear-cell, metastatic renal-cell carcinoma, in a 1:1 ratio, to receive a continuous dose of pazopanib (800 mg once daily; 557 patients) or sunitinib in 6-week cycles (50 mg once daily for 4 weeks, followed by 2 weeks without treatment; 553 patients).'

ORR - N (%) | Treatment | Overall.evidence: On page 5, under 'Tumor Response and Overall Survival', it states 'partial responses were observed in 170 patients in the pazopanib group (31%)' and 'Complete responses were observed in 1 patient in the pazopanib group'. PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The document reports the median progression-free survival duration (8.4 months) and the percentage of patients who experienced disease-progression events (60%) in the pazopanib group on page 4, under 'EFFICACY' -> 'Progression-free Survival'. However, it does not explicitly state an overall PFS rate as a percentage for the treatment arm. The hazard ratio (1.05) is a comparative measure between arms, not a rate for a single arm.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The document reports the median progression-free survival duration (9.5 months) and the percentage of patients who experienced disease-progression events (58%) in the sunitinib group on page 4, under 'EFFICACY' -> 'Progression-free Survival'. However, it does not explicitly state an overall PFS rate as a percentage for the control arm. The hazard ratio (1.05) is a comparative measure between arms, not a rate for a single arm.
